title: Service Request Fulfillment Time
spl:
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_request"
| eval fulfillment_hours=round((closed_at-opened_at)/3600,1)
| stats avg(fulfillment_hours) as avg_hours, median(fulfillment_hours) as median_hours by cat_item
| sort -avg_hours
description: Fulfillment time metrics drive service catalog optimization and customer satisfaction. Slow fulfillment reduces adoption.
value: Fulfillment time metrics drive service catalog optimization and customer satisfaction. Slow fulfillment reduces adoption.
grandmaExplanation: Fulfillment time metrics drive service catalog optimization and customer satisfaction ‚Äî so tickets, changes, and incidents are on track and nothing is silently missed.
implementation: Track service request lifecycle from submission to fulfillment. Calculate fulfillment time per catalog item. Identify items with slow fulfillment for automation opportunities. Report on catalog efficiency.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Service request data.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Track service request lifecycle from submission to fulfillment. Calculate fulfillment time per catalog item. Identify items with slow fulfillment for automation opportunities. Report on catalog efficiency.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_request"
| eval fulfillment_hours=round((closed_at-opened_at)/3600,1)
| stats avg(fulfillment_hours) as avg_hours, median(fulfillment_hours) as median_hours by cat_item
| sort -avg_hours
```

Understanding this SPL

**Service Request Fulfillment Time** ‚Äî Fulfillment time metrics drive service catalog optimization and customer satisfaction. Slow fulfillment reduces adoption.

Documented **Data sources**: Service request data. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:sc_request. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:sc_request". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **fulfillment_hours** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by cat_item** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (avg fulfillment by item), Table (catalog item performance), Line chart (fulfillment time trend).
visualization: Bar chart (avg fulfillment by item), Table (catalog item performance), Line chart (fulfillment time trend).
